title: GLR parser engine: parse loop and error recovery
description:
Implement the core GLR parsing engine — the main parse loop with shift/reduce/accept and error recovery. This is the hardest and most complex task in the project.

Depends on the data structures bead (Tree/Node/TreeCursor/GSS stack) and the corpus test runner (for test-driven development against real grammar expectations).

Deliverables:
- parser.go: Parser struct (language, lexer, stack, reusableNode, oldTree, finishedTree, tokenCache). Main parse loop (ts_parser__advance equivalent). Shift, reduce, accept actions. Token caching. context.Context integration for cancellation (check ctx.Done() in main loop).
- Parser.Reset() implementation: clear stack, reusable node state, token cache, finished tree.
- Error recovery: handle_error, recover, do_all_potential_reductions, condense_stack. Error cost constants (ERROR_COST_PER_RECOVERY=500, ERROR_COST_PER_MISSING_TREE=110, ERROR_COST_PER_SKIPPED_TREE=100, ERROR_COST_PER_SKIPPED_LINE=30, ERROR_COST_PER_SKIPPED_CHAR=1). MAX_VERSION_COUNT=6, MAX_COST_DIFFERENCE=1800.
- Integration: wire Parser to use Lexer (from pso bead) and Language (from 4q9 bead) to drive the full parse pipeline.

Tests:
- Parse JSON files using hand-compiled grammar, verify S-expression output matches C tree-sitter
- Run JSON corpus tests via the corpus test runner (from corpus bead)
- Test error recovery on malformed JSON
- Parse with context cancellation
- Test Parser.Reset() clears all state
- Multiple sequential parses on same parser
- GLR ambiguity: verify stack version splitting and merging

Reference: docs/design.md §6, §9 Phase 3, §10 Error Recovery Fidelity, C source: parser.c
